articles.title: Fake Notepad++ Plugin Delivers MATCHBOIL.V2 in UAC-0099 Attacks
articles.summary: The Computer Emergency Response Team of Ukraine (CERT-UA) has warned of a new campaign that involves the use of a malicious program that's dressed up as a Notepad++ plugin to compromise Windows systems. The activity has been attributed by the agency to a threat cluster it tracks as UAC-0099, a Russia-aligned group that has previously observed weaponizing security flaws in WinRAR software to

articles.title: ChatGPT AgentForger Flaw Could Deploy Rogue Workspace Agents via a Phishing Link
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical vulnerability in OpenAI's ChatGPT Workspace Agents that could have allowed a single phishing link to stealthily build, authorize, and deploy an autonomous artificial intelligence (AI) agent inside a victim's organization. The vulnerability has been codenamed AgentForger by Zenity Labs. The issue has since been addressed by OpenAI as of June 8,

articles.title: Claude Cowork Flaw Could Let AI Agent Escape Its VM and Access Mac Files
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a sandbox escape vulnerability in Anthropic's Claude Cowork that makes it possible to break out of the confines of a Linux virtual machine (VM) within which the agent runs to read or write files anywhere on the Mac. articles.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
articles.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Aid for Ukraine, drone attacks in Sudan, DR Congo deaths, neurological disorders in the Americas
articles.summary: UN partners launched the Winter Response Plan to help 2.1 million people in Ukraine survive another cold season as the country grapples with the on-going Russian invasion.

articles.title: US and Iran trade more strikes in Gulf as fears of escalation mount
articles.summary: It comes as Iran condemned the US plan to use frozen Iranian assets to pay for war damages.

articles.title: Wildfires and record dry conditions trigger drought in parts of Wales
articles.summary: Wales has seen about 5% of the average rainfall for July, after a series of back-to-back heatwaves.

articles.title: Gaza’s food gains could unravel without sustained aid, UN warns
articles.summary: Food security and child nutrition have improved across the Gaza Strip largely due to a scale-up in humanitarian assistance and commercial traffic, but progress remains fragile and could quickly reverse without sustained access, funding and recovery support.

articles.title: NodeBB Patches Eight AI-Found Flaws Exposing Admin Access and Private Chats
articles.summary: Eight security flaws in NodeBB went public on Wednesday, along with the code to exploit them. Aikido Security rates all eight as high severity and says its AI pentest agents found them in a six-hour review of the forum software's source code. Every version before 4.14.0 is affected. NodeBB has fixed them all, and administrators should be on 4.14.2. The simplest one takes a settings change. A

articles.title: ThreatsDay: Android Spyware, PLC Attacks, AI Image Prompt Injection + 12 More Stories
articles.summary: Most of this week's trouble came dressed as something useful. A package stole data. A fake extension opened remote access. A safety app became spyware. An image gave hidden orders to an AI agent. Other threats hid in open systems, weak code, and normal network traffic. The threats change every week. articles.title: Energy transition policymaking must evolve to fit an age of rupture
articles.summary: Economic competitiveness, energy security and green industrialisation are driving real-world change - but will this damage our collective mission for a fair green shift? articles.title: Hosepipe ban in force as drought threatens - is your area affected?
articles.summary: More than eight million households have been placed under hosepipe bans - which has raised questions about how effectively water resources are being managed.

articles.title: Look for NASA+ Now Streaming on Amazon Fire TV
articles.summary: Continuing agency efforts to bring space closer to home, NASA+ is heading to more streaming platforms. On Thursday, NASA announced its programming is on Fire TV Channels. articles.title: NASA’s MAVEN Illuminates New Understanding of Auroras at Mars
articles.summary: NASA MAVEN (Mars Atmosphere and Volatile Evolution) mission scientists have uncovered a key puzzle piece in understanding certain types of auroras on Mars, finding that they form in a similar way to Earth-based auroras. Results published Thursday in Nature Communications show the same mechanism that circulates and catapults charged particles into Earth’s atmosphere is happening at […]

articles.title: Kimi K3 Agents Found Redis Zero-Days and Built RCE Exploit, Researchers Say
articles.summary: Redis shipped seven security releases on July 23 after researchers published authenticated RCE PoCs for stock Redis 6.2.22, 7.4.9, 8.6.4, and 8.8.0. All four chains require RESTORE. The Streams chains also need EVAL and XGROUP; the 8.8.0 chain needs EVAL and the bundled RedisBloom module. Redis says the underlying memory flaws may lead to remote code execution. Redis 6.2.23, 7.2.15, and 7.4.10

articles.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
articles.summary: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor recovery. Opening the message was enough to start it. The NSA, CISA and partner agencies published

articles.title: Access to finance ‘strengthens climate resilience’ among sub-Saharan women
articles.summary: Empowering women through greater access to finance could “strengthen” households’ resilience to “climate shocks”, according to a new study. Published… The post Access to finance ‘strengthens climate resilience’ among sub-Saharan women appeared first on Carbon Brief .

articles.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot and near Madrid as wildfires spread
articles.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.

articles.title: US hits dozens of countries with new wave of tariffs
articles.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February.

articles.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters hold talks with government as stalemate continues
articles.summary: The meeting came hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike.

articles.title: Tanzanian lecturer arrested over anti-government protests charged with terrorism
articles.summary: He is among more than 130 people detained over planned protests calling for democratic reforms.

articles.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
articles.summary: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

articles.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
articles.summary: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

articles.title: Teen jailed for exam cheating in Guinea dies after sickle cell complications, father says
articles.summary: Mamadou Djouma Bah, 17, was arrested and sentenced to prison for exam fraud earlier this month.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: How to turn preventive diplomacy rhetoric into action
articles.summary: In the Security Council debate on Thursday on how best to settle disputes between States peacefully, ambassadors provided examples of success and challenges ahead under such themes as effective early warning and how to move from rhetorical support for preventive diplomacy to ensuring more consistent use of its Chapter VI powers under the UN Charter.

articles.title: Stranded seafarers remain trapped as Hormuz shipping stalls
articles.summary: As the Iran-US war continues, the key question remains unanswered about how shipping through the Strait of Hormuz will be managed in future – although the UN has reiterated that charging tolls for safe passage runs contrary to international law.

articles.title: Thousands prevented from returning home to southern Lebanon
articles.summary: As months of conflict continue to devastate communities across Lebanon, families are being left without shelter, healthcare and other essential services.

articles.title: NASA Astronaut Chris Williams Closes Out Space Station Mission
articles.summary: After eight months aboard the International Space Station for his first mission, NASA astronaut Chris Williams is preparing to return to Earth. articles.title: Seeing AI Agents Is Not Enough. Security Teams Must Enforce What They Can Do
articles.summary: AI agent security is moving through a familiar maturity curve: adoption, then visibility, and finally, control. But what we've collectively discovered is that enforcing least privilege for AI agents is harder than we ever imagined. articles.title: Most “zombie credits” locked out of new UN carbon market after China and India snub
articles.summary: Fears of a flood of environmentally-dubious carbon credits have faded, with most old projects not able to transition to new UN market The post Most “zombie credits” locked out of new UN carbon market after China and India snub appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Indonesian babies were trafficked to Singapore. What happens to them now?
articles.summary: At least 12 babies have been trafficked from Indonesia to Singapore, but what will happen to them remains unclear.

articles.title: South Africa's president wins bid to temporarily halt impeachment inquiry over 'Farmgate'
articles.summary: Ramaphosa has faced scrutiny following the theft of large sums of cash from his private farm in 2020.

articles.summary: Ben Rich looks at what is and isn't allowed during a hosepipe ban.

articles.title: Fate of massive 20m-high space radar in ministers' hands
articles.summary: The MoD wants to put 27 giant radar dishes in Pembrokeshire, which campaigners have strongly opposed.

articles.title: Great Apes kiss and cuddle to keep the peace, says study
articles.summary: Hugging before stressful events may be an ancient way of building trust, say scientists studying apes.

articles.title: Nicaragua continues to weaken political freedoms, UN warns
articles.summary: The UN human rights chief Volker Türk on Wednesday condemned new threats from Nicaragua’s co-president Daniel Ortega to hinder civil rights and prevent dissenting individuals and groups from participating in elections.

articles.title: Deadly Afghanistan floods kill at least 26 as aid agencies race to reach survivors
articles.summary: Flash floods have killed at least 26 people across eastern Afghanistan, including 23 in the mountainous province of Nuristan, where homes, markets and roads were swept away and search teams continued looking for people reported missing.

articles.summary: Crews at NASA’s Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans transport the 130-foot-tall Artemis IV liquid hydrogen tank out of a production cell inside the main factory building into a detached test building on a separate portion of the 829-acre site on May 15, 2026.
